title: Bill Deary at a Wayerhaeuser ball
description: Bill Deary compliments a countess at a Weyerhaeuser ball. He pays farmers for eggs broken on the train. He dictates a letter to his secretary.

title: Bill Helmer mistakes a bear for a person; man touches wild dear
description: Bill Helmer builds fires to keep warm while cruising timber. He mistakes a bear for a person at night. He tells off a mother bear with cubs. A man touches a wild deer in Leo's presence.

title: Salmon finding their way back home.
description: The magic way salmon find their way back home.

title: Cedar becoming vulnerable; blister rust
description: Cedar has become as valuable as white pine. The spread of blister rust.

title: Goes on a three day trip; cook gets stuck in cellar with a bear
description: Bill Helmer was secretive about his plans. Frank Mallory gives him a month's food for a three day trip. Dutch the cook gets stuck in the root cellar with a bear.

title: Travels the woods
description: Bill travels the woods: his answer to Leo's fear of getting lost. He remembers corners twenty years later.

title: Five Iron Men of Minnesota; John abandoned and rescued; John dies celebrating his rescue
description: Bill's brother John (Leo's father-in-law), one of the five Iron Men of Minnesota, is abandoned, and rescued by a trapper in the Canadian lakes' wilderness. Bill and another brother join in the search. John dies celebrating his rescue with his friends.

title: Old railroad car; boy scouts; surprise feast deep in the woods; scares scouts
description: A car-whacker checks loads of poles. Train wheels checked by hammering. An old railroad car for the boy scouts. A surprise feast prepared for the scouts deep in the woods. The scouts were a tough bunch. Leo scares scouts who want to dump a member into the creek.

title: Capture two grizzly bears; cub scalps a little girl; trained Russian bears
description: Bears. The scouts capture two grizzly cubs. A grown cub scalps a little girl. Martha, a cub, escapes, scares kids, and climbs a tree in Leo's yard. A woman shoots a pet bear turned vicious. Falling a tree in which a bear is hibernating. Russian bears wonderfully trained.

title: Movie theater owner paying for movies; gas engine goes out
description: How the movie theatre owner paid for his movies in hard times. His gas engine goes out and people wait in the dark theatre.

title: Bribing Perd Hughes, the sheriff, for extra hours
description: Bribing Perd Hughes, the sheriff, for extra hours of power plant light for night parties. Electricity goes on Wednesday during the day for ironing.

title: Pat Malone's duties; tracking abilities; prisoners cut hole in the jail
description: Pat Malone's duties and pay. Pat meets a somnambulist. His tracking abilities. Prisoners cut a hole in the jail and take leaves.

title: Drunk lumberjack didn't set house on fire
description: A drunk lumberjack proves he didn't set a house on fire.

title: Perd Hughes; whiskey discovered
description: More about Perd Hughes. His whiskey still described, discovered.

title: Pee Wee Eller outsmarts the cook
description: Pee Wee Eller outsmarts the cook who wants to "can" him. His cream puffs burst in lumberjack faces. A squaw offers to sell her husband.

title: Bootleggers go to college; hunt for hidden booze
description: Bootleggers go to "college" for nine months in jail. Deputy sheriffs impersonate jacks to buy booze. Hunting for hidden booze.

title: Bovill schoolmarm finds pork "astute"
description: A new Bovill schoolmarm finds the pork too "astute".

title: Drinkers conning bartenders
description: How drinkers con bartenders.

title: Camps serving good food
description: Why the camps serve good food.

title: Bovill cedar pole yard; work with cedar poles
description: The Bovill cedar pole yard. Leo argues over a rotten pole. Increment borer explained. Cat-face explained. Leo scaled 1008 poles one day. The yard setup explained. Leo cuts the end off a pole and fools a teamster. Creosoting the poles.

title: Tracks made by peg-legged man
description: The tracks made by a peg-legged man pushing a wheel barrow in the snow.

title: Cat-Face Ed's name; pole industry dead; cedar used for panelling
description: Why Leo gives Cat-Face Ed his name. Leo's creosote plugs are mistaken for a dead cat. Nutty woodpeckers peck good poles and smokestacks. Pole industry dead; cedar now used for panelling.
